commands.review_summary:
The PR bounds GitHub Copilot device-flow login success JSON reads with the shared provider JSON reader and adds regression coverage for normal, error, and oversized-response paths.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +205. Total +208 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and contributor proof, though I did not run tests in this read-only checkout. Current main still calls native `response.json()` on the shared device-flow success path, and the PR proof exercises an oversized streaming response through the changed path.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is identified on the current head; the remaining action is maintainer review of the auth-provider compatibility tradeoff and completed CI evidence.

target.title: Fix/bound copilot login json responses
target.body:
# fix(github-copilot): bound device-flow JSON response reads

## What Problem This Solves

`postGitHubDeviceFlowForm` in `extensions/github-copilot/login.ts` calls
`await response.json()` on success responses from both the device-code URL and
the access-token polling URL without any byte limit. An attacker who can intercept
or spoof the GitHub endpoint (MITM, DNS poisoning, or operator-misconfigured proxy)
could return an arbitrarily large JSON body and exhaust Node.js heap memory.

The fix is XS: one import line, two lines at the call site.

## Why This Change Was Made

`readProviderJsonResponse` (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`) caps JSON reads
at 16 MiB, cancels the underlying `ReadableStream` on overflow, and surfaces a
provider-tagged error. The same helper is already used in
`extensions/github-copilot/usage.ts` and `embeddings.ts` within the same extension —
no new dependency surface is added.

The non-OK branch (`!response.ok`) already throws before reading the body; only the
success-body read at line 151 was unbounded.

## User Impact

- **Normal path**: no change — valid GitHub device code and access token responses
  are well under 1 KiB. The full normal flow (device code → authorization → token)
  passes the regression suite unchanged.
- **Oversized / malicious response**: instead of OOM, the process now throws a
  `ProviderHttpError("github-copilot.device-flow: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes")`.

## Evidence

### Unit tests — `extensions/github-copilot/login.test.ts` (new file, 7 tests)

```
✓  runGitHubCopilotDeviceFlow — normal flow
   ✓ returns authorized status and access token on successful flow          22ms
   ✓ returns access_denied when GitHub rejects the authorization             2ms
   ✓ returns expired when GitHub reports expired_token                       1ms
✓  runGitHubCopilotDeviceFlow — HTTP error propagation
   ✓ throws with failureLabel on non-OK device code response                 1ms
   ✓ throws with failureLabel on non-OK access token response                2ms
✓  postGitHubDeviceFlowForm — response size bound
   ✓ bounds oversized device code body and cancels the stream                8ms
   ✓ bounds oversized access token body and cancels the stream               9ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
   Duration  1.98s
```

Tests use `setGitHubCopilotDeviceFlowFetchGuardForTesting` (the existing test seam)
to inject real `Response` objects without mocking any modules. Coverage:

| Scenario | Asserts |
|---|---|
| Full happy path | `{ status: "authorized", accessToken }`, `showCode` called with correct args, exactly 2 network calls |
| `access_denied` | `{ status: "access_denied" }` |
| `expired_token` | `{ status: "expired" }` |
| Device code HTTP 401 | throws `"GitHub device code failed: HTTP 401"` |
| Token poll HTTP 500 | throws `"GitHub device token failed: HTTP 500"` |
| Oversized device code body | throws `"github-copilot.device-flow"`, `readCount < 64`, `canceled === true` |
| Oversized access token body | throws `"github-copilot.device-flow"`, `readCount < 64`, `canceled === true` |

### Real-network proof (`node scripts/proof-copilot-login-bound.mjs`)

```
HTTP status: 200
Threw: github-copilot.device-flow: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes
Server sent: ~17.0 MiB of 64 MiB total
PASS: stream cancelled after ~17.0 MiB (cap ~16 MiB, not 64 MiB)
```

A local `node:http` server streams 64 MiB. The client read ~17 MiB (cap + TCP
buffer) before cancelling; the remaining ~47 MiB was never transferred.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/github-copilot/login.ts` | import `readProviderJsonResponse`; replace `await response.json()` |
| `extensions/github-copilot/login.test.ts` | new file — 7 tests covering full device flow, HTTP errors, and both oversized-body paths |
target.author: hugenshen
